entry.title: Writing Standards
entry.summary: This page defines the difference between Notes and Blog content.
entry.content:
This page defines the difference between Notes and Blog content.

Notes​

  • Goal: speed and utility
  • Verification level: lightweight
  • Status: may include incomplete or incorrect conclusions

Blog​

  • Goal: publish reliable technical writing
  • Verification level: stronger checking and revision
  • Expected quality: references, traceable reasoning, clearer claims

Practical Rule​

If an idea is still evolving, keep it in Notes.

If a topic is stable and can be defended with evidence, publish it as a Blog post.

entry.title: Site Scope and Content Policy
entry.summary: This website separates content into three tracks: Notes, Blog, and Projects.
entry.content:
This website separates content into three tracks: Notes, Blog, and Projects.

  • Notes are quick personal understanding and can contain mistakes.
  • Blog posts are formal publications with stronger validation.
  • Projects pages document implementation details and architecture decisions.

This separation keeps daily learning fast while preserving a reliable place for finalized ideas.
